text:
Straight-talking, cool and now, a history maker: Team GB's Keely Hodgkinson

‘Mission on,’ Keely Hodgkinson, 22, expressed, eyes on the prize, on the eve of the 800m Olympics final inParis, her baby purple, talon-like manicure catching the late Parisian sunlight. Well now, gold in the bag after blazing a trail round the Stade de France and its 80,000 strong audience, she can safely say: ‘mission accomplished’.

Take a glimpse inside the world of Princess Margaret’s youngest grandchild and her only granddaughter in this exclusive interview from the May 2023 issue ofTatler

Team GB’s straight-talking, prime-moving golden girl from Wigan was actually already a history-maker. At theTokyoOlympics, aged 19, she blasted Dame Kelly Holmes’ 800m record out the track – but still only achieved a silver. ‘I want to turn this into a goldone day,’ she said at the time. ‘I’d like some more bling.’ Well, bling she has secured – and now, as the first British track gold since Dame Jessica Ennis-Hill atLondon2012: the world is her oyster… and no doubt brands will be queueing up around the block for her endorsement.

Great Britain's Hodgkinson celebrates winning the Women's 800m Final

When she’s not on the track in her Nike sweats (she’s already their poster girl: ‘No guts. No gold. Just Do It’ which has already, at her Manchester HQ, been upgraded to: ‘All time greats don’t stop at gold’) she won’t be seen without herChanelsunglasses and Louis Vuitton handbag. With her Olympic pay day, she’s reportedly set her sights on a Porsche. As reported in theTimes, she said: ‘It’s going to sound so materialistic, I was like, “if I get the gold, it’s a nice payday and I will buy myself a new car”,’ she said. ‘“I wanted a Porsche.”’

With 367k Instagram followers and counting, the 22-year-old is an It girl in the making

With 367kInstagramfollowers and counting, it’s going to be a gold rush for any brand worth their salt. Indisputably, she’s the full package: she’s lofty and model-like, cool and committed, plus absolutelyfashion-adoring. ‘I get ready for a track meet as if I’m going out. It’s like a stage’ you’re preparing to perform,’ she toldEllemagazine. Legions of comments have been made on the Insta-videoof her gold medal-securing final sprint – which has now been liked nearly 100,000 times. It Girl, model and activist Adwoa Aboah wrote: ‘Absolute savage legend!!!!’

The nation, including Jessica Ennis-Hill and Gabby Logan in the BBC studio, had their nails in their teeth as Keely shot round the tracks last night, flaxen blonde ponytail flailing – at one point, her nearest competitor, Tsige Duguma from Kenya, loomed dangerously close. But the next second, Keely had shaken her off. Hodgkinson was leading from 300m and held it. Post-race, she embraced Lord Coe, the double Olympic gold medallist, before galloping around the stadium waving a Union Jack flag bearing the name Leigh Harriers, the running club she joined as a child –and who witnessed that gold ambition from the off.

The highest paid actress of the early Golden era, Greta Garbo lived a reclusive life, ever desperate for privacy and peace. Now, as a new documentary explores her life,Tatlerintroduces you to the enigma of the early cinema age

Hodgkinson's Instagram is full of glamorous shots and fashionable moments

But it hasn’t simply been golds, silvers and roses all the way for Hodgkinson. She is 95 per cent deaf in one ear following surgery in 2015 to remove a non-cancerous tumour. It went successfully, only disrupting training temporarily – and at 16 she won the English 800m under-20 title and the European under-18 gold, breaking the championship record. ‘It’s not too bad,’ she has previously said.

Hodgkinson celebrates as she wins the Women's 800m Final

The best part really is that Hodgkinson is only just getting started. At 22, she is already the sixth-fastest woman in history, and she’s got her sights set on beating that world record of 1 min 53.28 secs (yesterday she ran 1 min 56.72). She’s the girl next door with the golden touch – and her track potential is exhilarating.

Lofty and model-like, she is the full package

She was photographed at aBritish Vogueevent last year… no doubt magazine covers, modelling contracts and lucrative sponsorship deals await. Of course, success in athletics comes at a gruelling cost. In theTimes, she previously talked about pushing through the pain barrier and being physically sick by ‘just thinking this will all pay off one day.’ It sure has – Keely Hodgkinson has succeeded on the global stage, and where it truly counts. An icon is born.

This feature was first published in August 2024 and remains unedited.





© Condé Nast Britain 2025
title: The girl next door with the golden touch: Keely Hodgkinson is the undisputed queen of track as she is honoured with an MBE by Prince William | Tatler
author: Annabel Sampson
description: The northern queen secured her sought-after, sweated for, long-desired gold medal earlier this year before triumphing at Sports Personality of the Year. Now, she has received the royal seal of approval, with an MBE from Prince William
